purpose: First periodic head anchor. Gives a Bitcoin upper-time-bound ('existed no later than') to every Tier-A chained row from the boundary through this head - the coverage the genesis+gap anchors (id <= 16,827,536) do not extend to. One head anchor covers all prior chained rows via the hash chain.

chain_state.identity: chained = COUNT(*) - 16,827,536 -> 31,914,456 - 16,827,536 = 15,086,920 (holds exactly). NOTE: the id-space above the boundary has 40,820 holes (AUTOINCREMENT ids consumed by rolled-back pre-WAL collection cycles and deleted unchained orphans), so max(id)-based counts overcount; COUNT(*) is the canonical identity. No chained row is missing - the full walk links every chained row.
chain_state.walk_output: total=31,914,456 chained=15,086,920 null_after_boundary=0 boundary_first=16827537 max_ts=2026-07-18T11:57:08.717617+00:00 | CHAIN OK: 15,086,920 rows from genesis; head=3364249fce72b662 (170.0s) | HEALTH: GREEN

verification.method: _snapshot_verify.py (PRIMARY): full-chain recompute from genesis a5989faa... over the static host snapshot, single-pass, mode=ro; every stored hash recomputed, every prev-link checked, boundary row links to the genesis corpus hash.
verification.snapshot: CPX52:/home/deploy/kalshi_dashboard/data/kalshi_signals_snapshot.db (produced by the /10 stepped-.backup sync pipeline, journal_mode=DELETE on dest, tail-linked sanity-checked)
verification.head_row_consistency: the stored hash of the top chained row (SELECT ... ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1) equals the recomputed walk head - the walk verifies every stored hash including the head row's, so head_hash above is the recomputed chain head, not merely a stored value.

coverage.this_head: all 15,086,920 chained rows, ids 16,827,537..31,955,276 - THIS record. Any rewrite of any chained row at or below the head breaks the recomputed chain against head_hash.
coverage.after_this_head: rows with id > 31,955,276 are chain-linked back to this head and await the next periodic head anchor.

semantics: The chain hash uses no secret -> public verifiability (anyone can recompute the walk from the data alone). It is THIS external anchor that pins time: content whose recomputed chain head equals head_hash existed no later than the Bitcoin attestation of this record. Secret-lessness alone does not stop author backdating; the Bitcoin anchor does.
ots_status: Stamped 2026-07-18 via public OpenTimestamps calendar servers; the accompanying .ots proof is the authoritative list of calendar commitments actually obtained. Bitcoin attestation pending calendar aggregation at commit time. Upgrade the .ots via ots_upgrade.py (typically hours), same flow as the genesis/gap proofs (both since attested on-chain).
